title: A Stable Right-Sided Quaternion Fourier Regime for Multichannel Spectral Analysis
abstract: This paper defines a stable right-sided discrete quaternion Fourier regime for multichannel spectral analysis. Building on a canonical quaternion lane representation of paired complex channels, it treats each lifted lane as a quaternion-valued sequence $x[n]\in\mathbb{H}$ and adopts a fixed transform axis $i$. The forward transform is $X[k]=\sum_{n=0}^{N-1}x[n]\exp\!\left(-i\,2\pi nk/N\right)$, with inverse $x[n]=(1/N)\sum_{k=0}^{N-1}X[k]\exp\!\left(+i\,2\pi nk/N\right)$. The contribution is not to survey every quaternion Fourier convention, but to make one public, reproducible choice for downstream WaveEngine-style processing. The paper proves exact inversion, right-linearity over the commuting slice subalgebra $\mathbb{C}_i$, nonnegativity of spectral power, and a Parseval identity $\sum_n\lvert x[n]\rvert^2=(1/N)\sum_k\lvert X[k]\rvert^2$. These results justify the fixed-axis right-sided transform as a deterministic spectral seam for quaternion-lifted multichannel data. The paper does not claim universal superiority over other quaternion Fourier conventions, does not address two-sided or higher-dimensional transforms, and does not yet develop the coherence metrics that sit on top of this spectral regime.
pdf_url: papers/qsp-002-right-sided-quaternion-fourier-transform/2. Stable Right-Sided Quaternion Fourier Regime for Multichannel Spectral Analysis.pdf
notes: Draft spectral-foundation paper defining the fixed-axis right-sided quaternion Fourier seam used after canonical quaternion lifting. This version proves inversion and Parseval-style invariants but intentionally defers cross-lane coherence and orientation metrics to the next paper in the series.
